action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 crashed with the same RuntimeError (64970 vs 65536) because model.py was never actually rewritten; it still passed flattened rays [1,65536,3] to the renderer. When some rays miss the bounding box, the renderer's internal filtering seems to mismatch shapes. Analysis: In earlier exploration (iteration 8), passing image-shaped rays [1,H,W,3] to the renderer worked for the internal get_spherical_cameras path. We should do the same for our custom rays: keep them as [1,H,W,3], let the renderer's forward() handle flattening, filtering, and scattering internally, then receive a full image back. Decision: Rewrite model.py to return rays in [1,H,W,3] shape, handle multiple possible renderer output dict keys robustly, run a quick local test on one validation object, then submit eval for baseline LPIPS and time measurement.
